question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that stake values are sourced from nodeStakeRepository.findAllStakeByConsensusTimestamp() at a fixed snapshot timestamp, and by timing the query to coincide with a nodeStake table update, receive a mix of old and new stake values that cause fee calculations to be inconsistent with design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the description field contains excessively long strings from the database, causing protobuf message bloat that degrades throughput of the fee-relevant node data stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() does not enforce any per-client rate limiting, allowing them to flood the gRPC endpoint with requests that exhaust database connection pool resources used by fee schedule queries, causing fee data to be unavailable to legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a FileID with fileNum=0 to EntityId.of(), causing EntityId.of() to create an entity with ID 0 that does not match the valid address book file IDs (101 or 102), triggering an INVALID_FILE_ID exception that is mapped to a gRPC INVALID_ARGUMENT status, and by doing this at scale, denying fee-relevant node data to legitimate clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the nodeAccountId returned by toAccountID() has a different shard or realm than expected, causing fee-routing clients to construct transactions with incorrect node account IDs and pay fees to wrong accounts outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the gRPC getNodes() endpoint to enumerate all node addresses and their stake values, then use this information to identify low-stake nodes with lower fee collection and preferentially route transactions to them to minimize fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that the ServiceEndpoint built in toNodeAddress() uses s.getDomainName() directly without validation, and if the database contains a malformed domain name, cause clients to fail DNS resolution and fall back to fee routing that bypasses intended node selection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the port value in ServiceEndpoint is set to 0 or a privileged port number from the database, causing clients to fail to connect to nodes and route fee-bearing transactions through alternative paths outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive stream's onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) to cause any database exception during node retrieval to be silently converted to a gRPC status error, preventing clients from detecting that the node list is incomplete and causing them to compute fees based on a partial node set?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative limit value in AddressBookQuery (e.g., limit=-1) to test whether the @Min(0) constraint on AddressBookFilter.limit is enforced before or after the reactive chain executes, and if validation is deferred, cause unexpected behavior in the .take() operator that affects fee-relevant node data delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the stake field is Long.MAX_VALUE due to a database corruption or injection, causing fee-routing clients to treat one node as having infinite stake and route all fee-bearing transactions to it, concentrating fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gRPC stream uses responseObserver::onNext for each node entry, and by sending a getNodes() request with limit=0 against a large address book, cause the server to buffer all NodeAddress objects in memory before the client reads them, leading to heap exhaustion that disrupts fee schedule services?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() when the database contains an IPv6 address string in the ipAddressV4 field, causing InetAddress.getByName() to return a 16-byte address instead of 4 bytes, resulting in a malformed ByteString that causes clients to fail to connect to nodes and route fees incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the nodeId field is set to a value that does not correspond to any entry in the nodeStakeMap, causing the stake to be overridden to 0 and fee-routing clients to treat that node as having no stake, skewing fee distribution outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getNodes() request and then hold the gRPC stream open indefinitely without reading responses, causing the server-side reactive pipeline to back-pressure and block database connections, preventing fee schedule data from being refreshed for other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a FileID with extremely large shard, realm, or fileNum values to EntityId.of(), causing integer overflow or unexpected entity ID construction that bypasses the valid file ID check in NetworkServiceImpl and returns node data from an unintended address book affecting fee parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the memo field contains non-UTF-8 bytes stored in the database, causing ByteString.copyFromUtf8() to produce a corrupted ByteString that causes protobuf deserialization errors on the client side, preventing correct fee routing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() does not validate the gRPC message size of the incoming AddressBookQuery, and by sending a crafted oversized protobuf message, cause the server to spend excessive CPU on deserialization, degrading fee schedule query performance?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where both domainName and ipAddressV4 are set in the same ServiceEndpoint, causing ambiguity in client-side node resolution that leads to fee-bearing transactions being routed to unintended endpoints outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive Mono.fromCallable() wrapping of toFilter() to cause a thread-blocking operation inside the callable that ties up a reactor thread, reducing the throughput of fee-relevant gRPC services for other clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the nodeAccountId.toAccountID() returns an account ID with shard=0, realm=0, accountNum=0, causing fee-routing clients to construct transactions with an invalid node account ID and have fees rejected or misrouted outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated getNodes() requests with fileId=101 and fileId=102 alternately to compare the two address books and identify discrepancies in stake values between them, using this information to determine which address book version leads to lower fees and exploit the difference outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() when the database contains a multicast IP address string in the ipAddressV4 field, causing InetAddress.getByName() to return a multicast address that clients interpret as a valid node endpoint, routing fee-bearing transactions to a multicast group outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive NodeAddress entries where the stake field is negative (due to a database anomaly), causing fee-routing clients to compute negative fee weights and behave outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS enforcement on the gRPC endpoint to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the getNodes() response stream, injecting modified NodeAddress entries with altered stake values that cause clients to compute fees outside design parameters?
